task_completion.description: Verify the tiddler is created with the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title of the tiddler is exactly 'Invoice Dashboard'.

task_completion.description: Verify the tiddler has the correct tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tag pills for 'Dashboard' and 'Finance' are visible just below the tiddler title.

task_completion.description: Verify the required overview text is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Overview of all client invoices.' is visible in the body of the tiddler, above the table.

task_completion.description: Verify the HTML table has the correct headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table is rendered with exactly five column headers in this order: 'Invoice ID', 'Client', 'Due Date', 'Amount', and 'Status'.

task_completion.description: Verify the table is populated with invoice data and formatted correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The table contains exactly 15 rows of data. The 'Invoice ID' column contains clickable links (usually blue text). The 'Amount' column values are prepended with a '$' sign.

task_completion.description: Verify the table rows are sorted by Due Date in descending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dates in the 'Due Date' column are ordered from the newest/latest date at the top to the oldest date at the bottom.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TiddlyWiki web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the TiddlyWiki application running in a web browser with standard UI elements (sidebar, edit buttons, save buttons) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the table is generated dynamically using the <$list> widget and not hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the edit mode is visible in any screenshot, the text area should contain TiddlyWiki widget syntax (e.g., <$list filter=...>) rather than 15 blocks of hardcoded HTML <tr> and <td> tags containing specific company names or amounts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just copy-paste a static image or mock table.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table looks like a native HTML table rendered within the TiddlyWiki tiddler body, and the links in the first column appear as genuine TiddlyWiki internal links.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target tiddler must be titled 'Invoice Dashboard' and tagged with 'Dashboard' and 'Finance'. The table must have exactly these headers: 'Invoice ID', 'Client', 'Due Date', 'Amount', 'Status'. There are exactly 15 synthetic invoices seeded in the environment, so the rendered table must display exactly 15 rows. The table must be dynamically generated; if the tiddler's raw text/edit mode is visible, it must NOT contain hardcoded strings like 'Altus Health Systems', 'NovaTech Manufacturing', '4500.00', or '8450.00'.
